- Our position was a step beyond their strong discouragement for any adhesive anchors in sustained tension to an outright prohibition. Current default choice would be Hilti undercut anchors, as they correspond to traditional ACI anchor failure cone theory.
- There have been significant changes in the ACI regarding adhesive anchors as they now have provisions for sustain tension, which reduce the allowable stress to minimize creep.
- But as we are all aware, it is
- installation
- ,
- testing
- and
- inspection
- that is key to a successful application so if using adhesive anchors, I always incorporate the
- ACI and ICC-ES qualification

- NZS 3101 cl. 10.4.6.5.2 requires column reinforcement to be anchored at least 3/4 of the cap depth from the face.
- From AS 3600 cl 13.1.4 the development length for a headed bar is 0.4 times that of a straight bar. (For 32dia bar - 0.4x1270=508mm)
- From NZS 3101 cl. 10.4.6.5.1 anchorage is taken to commence 8db from the face of the cap, ie 256mm, giving a minimum embedment from the face of the cap of approx. 765mm to the underside of the anchor.

_Captured Thursday, 16 February 2023 5:20 PM · General › General / Misc_

- Hi, Could you please call me when you are available for a quick chat?
- There is an increasing interest in timber bridges here in New Zealand.
- We are trying to put together a capability profile for the design of timber bridges that can be presented to the clients. We want to demonstrate the skill and knowledge we have within the WSP team both nationally and internationally for the design of vehicular or pedestrian timber bridges.
- We want to present a team to the client that can work on the upcoming project to develop solutions best suits the project requirements.
- I would appreciate it if you could send us a project profile for some sample timber bridge projects and the people involved in the design process.
